response: Alex explored various perspectives through novels that challenged their traditional beliefs. Each novel took a specific number of days to finish. The first book had 120 pages and required 4 days of reading. The second one had 180 pages and took 6 days to complete. The third novel, the longest, had 240 pages and was finished in 8 days. Alex read consistently, finishing the same number of pages each day for each novel. To find the average pages per day, the total pages need to be divided by the total days. The total number of pages read across all three novels is 120 plus 180 plus 240. Adding these together gives a total of 540 pages. The total number of days Alex spent reading is 4 days plus 6 days plus 8 days. That totals to 18 days. Dividing the total pages by the total days gives the average pages per day. 540 divided by 18 equals 30. So, on average, Alex read 30 pages per day across all three novels. This average combines the different reading rates for each novel. The first novel had 120 pages over 4 days, which means 30 pages per day. The second novel had 180 pages over 6 days, which also means 30 pages per day. The third novel had 240 pages over 8 days, which again is 30 pages per day. This consistency across all three novels is what leads to the average of 30 pages per day. Alex maintained a steady pace for each book individually. The first novel was shorter and took fewer days to complete. The second was longer but still maintained the same daily reading rate. The third, being the longest, required more days but the same daily rate. This shows a disciplined approach to reading. Each novel had its own length and duration. The daily reading amount remained the same for each. This consistency is what allows for the average to be calculated so easily. The average is not affected by the differences in length or duration. It is based purely on the total pages and total days. This makes the calculation straightforward and accurate. Alex’s reading schedule was well-planned and consistent. The novels varied in length, but the daily reading rate was the same. This consistency helped Alex finish each book on time. The average is a reflection of the daily effort Alex put in.
